intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The biome is in a state of advanced decay or total loss. The expected specimens (P1-P4) are not visually identifiable as living botanical entities. The white rabbit anchor is present but the surrounding soil is devoid of viable plant matter.

plant_audit.p3_black_pot: Contains white rabbit anchor; no visible Pothos foliage.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears desiccated and dark; no signs of active growth or moisture.
biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of a metal spoon in the T-2 image suggests human intervention/maintenance attempt, but no subsequent recovery is observed.
biome_observations.debris: Minimal organic debris; no fungal blooms or moss detected.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t3: Initial state shows minimal structure; possible trace of a sprout in P3.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: Introduction of a metal spoon in the yellow pot (P1/P2 area).
temporal_deltas.t2_to_current: Complete loss of all identifiable plant structures. The environment has transitioned to a static, non-biological state.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is non-viable. All specimens have suffered total systemic collapse. No photosynthetic activity is detected.
anomalies: Complete disappearance of all plant matter over the 5-day observation window.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the botanical collection. Starting from a state of minimal visibility, the plants have either been removed or have withered completely into the soil. The presence of a spoon in the T-2 image indicates an attempt at soil manipulation or feeding, but this did not prevent the total loss of the specimens. The current state is a barren desk environment.
